name: Bacterial Red Disease
cause: Usually caused by poor water quality, overcrowding, and stress that allow opportunistic bacteria to infect fish skin and blood vessels.
symptoms: Red patches on the body, inflamed fins, skin hemorrhage, sluggish movement, and reduced feeding.
treatment: Isolate infected fish, improve aeration, and apply veterinarian-recommended antibacterial treatment in feed or bath.
prevention: Maintain clean water, avoid overstocking, disinfect equipment, and provide balanced feed to strengthen immunity.

name: Bacterial Aeromoniasis
cause: Infection by Aeromonas species in warm water conditions, especially when fish are stressed or injured.
symptoms: Ulcers, swollen abdomen, scale loss, fin rot, and occasional sudden mortality in ponds.
treatment: Move affected fish to a treatment tank, sanitize water, and follow approved antibiotic protocol from aquaculture experts.
prevention: Regular pond hygiene, good nutrition, stress reduction during handling, and periodic health monitoring.

name: Bacterial Gill Disease
cause: High organic load and low dissolved oxygen promote bacterial growth on gill tissues.
symptoms: Rapid breathing, fish gasping near water surface, swollen or pale gills, and weak activity.
treatment: Enhance water exchange, increase aeration, reduce organic waste, and use recommended antibacterial bath treatment.
prevention: Keep dissolved oxygen stable, avoid excess feed waste, and perform regular pond bottom cleaning.

name: Fungal Disease - Saprolegniasis
cause: Saprolegnia fungi infect fish with skin injuries or eggs exposed to poor environmental conditions.
symptoms: Cotton-like white or gray growth on skin, fins, or eggs, along with skin irritation and lethargy.
treatment: Separate infected stock, apply antifungal bath as advised, and remove dead eggs or tissue from tanks.
prevention: Avoid physical injuries, keep water quality stable, quarantine new fish, and disinfect hatchery tools.

name: Healthy Fish
cause: No active disease detected in the selected image based on current mock prediction.
symptoms: Clear skin, normal swimming, active feeding behavior, and no visible lesions or discoloration.
treatment: No treatment required. Continue routine observation and good husbandry practices.
prevention: Sustain quality feed, regular water testing, biosecurity practices, and timely pond maintenance.

name: Parasitic Disease
cause: External or internal parasites spread through contaminated water, infected fish, or poor pond sanitation.
symptoms: Frequent rubbing against pond surfaces, visible spots, fin damage, weight loss, and restlessness.
treatment: Use species-safe antiparasitic treatment, improve filtration, and isolate heavily infested fish.
prevention: Quarantine new fish, maintain biosecurity, monitor stocking density, and clean ponds regularly.

name: Viral Disease - White Tail Disease
cause: Caused by viral infection, often spreading quickly in hatcheries and juvenile culture systems.
symptoms: Whitish tail muscle, abnormal swimming, muscle weakness, poor feeding, and high mortality in fry.
treatment: No specific antiviral cure; provide supportive care, remove infected fish, and disinfect affected systems.
prevention: Use pathogen-free seed, strict hatchery hygiene, regular screening, and control movement between culture units.
